cat_advanced_inputs_desc: Input components for complex data formats or special interactions.
cat_alerts_desc: Components for communicating important information or feedback to users.
cat_app_shell_desc: Core structural components that define the application's top-level layout and identity.
cat_basic_inputs_desc: Components for basic text and numeric input.
cat_buttons_desc: Fundamental components for triggering actions and navigation.
cat_data_containers_desc: Components designed to encapsulate and organize content within distinct boundaries.
cat_data_indicators_desc: Components for visually communicating data status or information.
cat_data_structures_desc: Components for displaying large volumes of data in structured and readable formats.
cat_form_layout_desc: Auxiliary components for structuring, labeling, and organizing form fields.
cat_internal_desc: Internal building blocks used to construct higher-level public components.
cat_layout_desc: Foundational components for controlling the positioning and spacing of elements.
cat_loading_desc: Visual indicators to inform users about background processes and data loading states.
cat_media_desc: Components for embedding and controlling visual and auditory media content.
cat_nav_elements_desc: Components that define the application structure and assist in navigation.
cat_nav_utilities_desc: Helper components that provide additional navigation-related functionality like scroll-to-top.
cat_overlays_desc: Temporary UI elements that appear over the main content for focused interaction or information.
cat_pickers_desc: Interactive components for selecting values from ranges, dates, or color palettes.
cat_selection_desc: Form controls for making choices among multiple options, such as checkboxes and switches.
cat_typography_desc: Foundational components for consistent text styling, hierarchy, and iconic representation.
cat_utilities_desc: A collection of miscellaneous helper components for scrolling, error handling, and more.
cat_visualization_desc: Components for translating complex data sets into intuitive and interactive charts.
guide_colors_border_desc: Used to show element boundaries or separators.
guide_colors_contrast_checker_desc: You can check if background and text color combinations meet accessibility standards.
guide_colors_contrast_text_desc: Colors for text and icons placed on top of semantic colors (backgrounds).
guide_colors_customization_desc: If you want to change or add color schemes, edit the following files:
guide_colors_customization_pccs_desc: Defines specific color codes based on hue and tone (e.g., $pccs-v1).
guide_colors_customization_semantic_desc: Defines colors for specific purposes (Primary, Destructive, etc.) and dark mode color schemes.
guide_colors_desc: A guide to the colors used in the WIM UI design system. This system is designed based on PCCS (Practical Color Co-ordinate System) for consistent color design.
guide_colors_pccs_desc: WIM UI internally maintains a PCCS color palette, allowing for intuitive color selection based on tones.
guide_colors_pccs_file_link: Refer to src/tokens/_pccs-colors.scss and _semantic-colors.scss for detailed color codes.
guide_colors_pccs_mapping_desc: wim UI manages colors as SCSS variables using PCCS notation (e.g., $pccs-v1, $pccs-gy8-5) and assigns them to semantic CSS variables.
guide_colors_scrollbar_desc: Colors used for scrollbars, such as in the ScrollArea component.
guide_colors_semantic_desc: Colors defined based on use or meaning, used to indicate state or role.
guide_colors_surface_desc: Colors used for application backgrounds and component surfaces, switching automatically in dark mode.
guide_colors_text_desc: Colors used for text, chosen for contrast against backgrounds.
guide_composition_map_desc: Mapping of which components major components use (wrap) internally. By combining lower-tier components, we provide consistent functionality.
guide_internal_desc: Guidelines for internal building blocks used to ensure consistency across high-level components.
guide_label_std_concept_desc: Ensures users can visually organize information by choosing the appropriate component based on the type of information and the presence of interaction.
guide_label_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that supplement information attributes or states, such as badges, tags, and chips.
guide_label_std_note_desc: Use 'Badge' for numerical counts, 'Tag' for information classification, and 'Chip' for manipulating choices.
guide_label_std_rule_2_desc: Unifies semantic colors (Success, Warning, Error, etc.) to ensure consistent meaning of information.
guide_label_std_rule_3_desc: Differentiates usage: Badges/Tags are non-interactive, while Chips are interactive (removable/selectable).
guide_label_std_rule_4_desc: When using icons, combine them with label text or provide appropriate Aria attributes.
guide_layout_desc: Guidelines for roles and placement of Header, Sidebar, Main, and Footer.
guide_layout_footer_content: Recommended: Copyright, terms of service, privacy policy, language switcher, social links
guide_layout_footer_desc: Contains supplementary or legal information. Often omitted in web applications but critical for websites.
guide_layout_header_content: Recommended: Logo, global search, notifications, user profile, top-level navigation
guide_layout_header_desc: Provides context for the entire application and access to global actions. Should include features accessible from anywhere.
guide_layout_main_desc: The central area where users perform tasks. Data display and operations happen here.
guide_layout_overlay_desc: Layer for catching user attention or inputting supplementary info without interrupting the workflow.
guide_layout_pageheader_content: Recommended: Page title, breadcrumbs, primary action buttons, filter toggles
guide_layout_pageheader_desc: Contains the current page title, breadcrumbs, and primary actions for that page (create, save, delete, etc.).
guide_layout_responsive_desc: Adapts the layout based on screen size. Typically, sidebars become drawers and header menus collapse into hamburger menus on mobile.
guide_layout_responsive_mobile: Mobile (< 768px): Sidebar -> Drawer, Header Menu -> Hamburger
guide_layout_rightpanel_content: Recommended: Property panels, comment threads, chat, help documentation
guide_layout_rightpanel_desc: Displays details of selected items, settings, help, or notification history. Can be permanent or temporarily triggered.
guide_layout_sidebar_content: Recommended: Main menu, project lists, filtering options, category lists
guide_layout_sidebar_desc: Manages primary navigation within the application and hierarchical movement. Suitable for drill-down menus.
guide_list_item_std_desc: When displaying user-defined options (dropdown items, menus, comboboxes, etc.), use the internal component BaseListItem to maintain a consistent look & feel (padding, hover color, click hit area) across the entire product.
guide_list_item_std_note_desc: If you are creating a new component that includes a list of selectable items, please reuse BaseListItem located in src/components/_internal/BaseListItem.
guide_list_item_std_reason_desc: Previously, each component manually implemented items via div or li, leading to inconsistent hit areas and spacing between icons and text. Unifying these improves the 'tangibility' of the UI and the overall product quality.
guide_list_item_std_rule_1_desc: Do not specify padding or background-color (hover) in custom SCSS.
guide_list_item_std_rule_2_desc: Use src/components/_internal/BaseListItem when rendering selectable items.
guide_list_item_std_rule_3_desc: Ensure --wim-color-bg-hover is applied for hover states (automatically handled by BaseListItem).
guide_list_item_std_rules_list: 1. Do not specify custom padding or background-color (hover) in your SCSS.\n2. Use src/components/_internal/BaseListItem when displaying items.\n3. Ensure the --wim-color-bg-hover token is always applied for hover colors (this is automatic if you use BaseListItem).
guide_lvb_a11y_keyboard_screenreader: Using the correct tag conveys the appropriate interaction method (Enter vs Space key) to users relying on assistive technologies like screen readers.
guide_lvb_a11y_rightclick_desc: `<a>` allows 'Open in new tab', but `<button>` does not.
guide_lvb_antipattern_text: Avoid stuffing JS logic into `<a>` via onclick, or using window.location.href in `<button>` for navigation.
guide_lvb_confusion_submit_text: For form submission buttons (e.g., 'Subscribe', 'Save'), `<button type='submit'>` is the best practice. It clearly defines the intent to 'submit data' and is correctly announced by screen readers.
guide_lvb_criteria_text: If the URL (address bar) changes after clicking, use `<a>`. If something completes on the spot, use `<button>`.
guide_lvb_desc: Even if they appear as the same 'button', HTML tags (`<a>` or `<button>`) must be used correctly according to their function and purpose.
guide_lvb_standard_text: Using `<a>` for page transitions and `<button>` for data submission or state changes is the correct approach in Web Standards, benefiting both accessibility and SEO.
guide_lvb_summary_text:
Want to go to another URL? → `<a>`
Want to send a form or change something? → `<button>`
guide_lvb_table_scene_a: Go to details page, Go to external site
guide_lvb_table_scene_b: Add to cart, Submit form, Open modal
guide_media_desc: Guidelines for using image, video, and audio components.
guide_pccs_contrast_desc: You can check if PCCS color combinations meet accessibility standards.
guide_pccs_customization_desc: If you want to change specific color codes in the PCCS palette, edit the following file:
guide_pccs_customization_note: * Changing this file will update semantic colors (Primary, Success, etc.) and component colors that reference these colors all at once.
guide_pccs_desc: A list of the PCCS color palette, which forms the basis of WIM UI's color design. Categorized by tone (combination of lightness and saturation) to support intuitive color design.
guide_range_std_concept_desc: Provides a UI that allows intuitive manipulation of continuous value sets and enables accurate value entry and verification.
guide_range_std_desc: Defines common rules for components where a user selects values from within a specific range, such as sliders and calendars.
guide_range_std_note_desc: Use 'Slider' for numeric values and 'Calendar' for dates. If extremely precise values are required from a wide range, consider using an 'InputNumber' field in conjunction.
guide_range_std_rule_1_desc: Manage `value` (single value) or `range` (start/end value pair) in a consistent prop format.
guide_range_std_rule_2_desc: Set `min` (minimum) and `max` (maximum) to restrict selections within the valid range.
guide_range_std_rule_3_desc: Present current selection values in real-time through tooltips or numeric displays during dragging or selection.
guide_range_std_rule_4_desc: Consider fine-tuning with keyboard arrow keys and combining with direct input (where applicable).
guide_selection_std_concept_desc: Achieves smooth view switching and filtering by enhancing the visibility of the selected state and ensuring accessibility, including keyboard operations.
guide_selection_std_desc: Defines common rules for components where a user selects one (or more) from several options, such as tabs and segmented controls.
guide_selection_std_note_desc: Use 'Tabs' for switching content and 'Segmented Control' for filtering or switching settings.
guide_selection_std_rule_1_desc: Use `value` and `onChange` (or `onValueChange`) to manage the current selection state.
guide_selection_std_rule_2_desc: Apply sliders or background color changes to the selected item to clearly indicate the active one.
guide_selection_std_rule_3_desc: Support moving between items with arrow keys and making selections with the Enter/Space keys.
guide_selection_std_rule_4_desc: Set `tablist` (for switching tabs) or `radiogroup` (for selecting settings) appropriately based on context.
guide_sequence_std_concept_desc: Visualizes the flow of information in chronological or process order, allowing users to intuitively grasp 'where they are now' and 'what lies in the past or future.'
guide_sequence_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that communicate ordered information or progress, such as Stepper and Timeline.
guide_sequence_std_note_desc: Use 'Stepper' for step-by-step progress involving user input or actions, and 'Timeline' for listing histories or events.
guide_sequence_std_rule_1: Unified Connectors: Link items with lines (Connectors) to explicitly show information continuity.
guide_sequence_std_rule_1_desc: Link items with lines (Connectors) to explicitly show information continuity.
guide_sequence_std_rule_2: Status Representation: Express the status of each step (Wait, Process, Finish, Error) using colors and icons for easy identification of progress.
guide_sequence_std_rule_2_desc: Express the status of each step (Wait, Process, Finish, Error) using colors and icons for easy identification of progress.
guide_sequence_std_rule_3: Layout Flexibility: Support both horizontal and vertical directions, allowing selection based on screen space and information density.
guide_sequence_std_rule_3_desc: Support both horizontal and vertical directions, allowing selection based on screen space and information density.
guide_sequence_std_rule_4: Content Structure: Each item basic configuration consists of three elements: 'Icon/Point', 'Title', and 'Description'.
guide_sequence_std_rule_4_desc: Each item basic configuration consists of three elements: 'Icon/Point', 'Title', and 'Description'.
guide_spacings_desc: Spacings and Border Radius definitions to maintain consistent layouts and shapes.
guide_spacings_focus_desc: The stroke width and offset spacing of the outline displayed on focus to ensure accessibility (such as clarifying the current location during keyboard operation).
guide_spacings_guideline_1: The base 1rem follows the browser default (usually 16px).
guide_spacings_guideline_2: Use CSS variables like var(--spacing-md) for spacings; do not use direct numerical values.
guide_spacings_guideline_3: Use different border radii depending on the component size (Button: sm/md, Card: lg).
guide_spacings_margin_desc: Use CSS variables like `var(--spacing-md)`, do not hardcode values.
guide_spacings_radius_desc: Use according to component size (Button: sm/md, Card: lg).
guide_state_std_concept_desc: Presents information so that users can correctly perceive the current system status and decide what to do next without hesitation.
guide_state_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that communicate specific screen-wide states, such as EmptyState and Result.
guide_state_std_note_desc: It is important not to just say 'No data,' but to suggest a 'solution' like clearing filters or creating new content.
guide_state_std_rule_1: Information Hierarchy: Organize information in the order of Title, Description, and Action Button to optimize eye-tracking.
guide_state_std_rule_1_desc: Organize information in the order of Title, Description, and Action Button to optimize eye-tracking.
guide_state_std_rule_2: Visual Feedback: Use icons and colors (Success, Error, Info, etc.) appropriate for the situation, ensuring the meaning is clear without relying on language alone.
guide_state_std_rule_2_desc: Use icons and colors (Success, Error, Info, etc.) appropriate for the situation, ensuring the meaning is clear without relying on language alone.
guide_state_std_rule_3: Providing the Next Action: Offer means for users to solve problems or continue working through the `action` or `extra` properties.
guide_state_std_rule_3_desc: Offer means for users to solve problems or continue working through the `action` or `extra` properties.
guide_state_std_rule_4: Translation Support: Allow translation keys for titles and descriptions to facilitate multi-language support.
guide_state_std_rule_4_desc: Allow translation keys for titles and descriptions to facilitate multi-language support.
guide_std_cat_display_desc: Standards for organizing information and conveying meaning accurately.
guide_std_cat_feedback_desc: Standards for providing appropriate feedback on system status to users.
guide_std_cat_input_desc: Standards to enable users to operate intuitively without confusion.
guide_std_cat_layout_desc: Standards that define the overall structure and consistent placement of applications.
guide_std_next_steps_desc: Individual standardization documents summarize component-by-component comparisons, accessibility status, and specific Do/Don'ts. Always refer to the corresponding standard when adding new components or modifying existing ones.
guide_std_overview_desc: At wimui, we maintain strict standards for both design and implementation to provide a consistent user experience. This page serves as an index to oversee these standardization documents and understand our design philosophy.
guide_std_pillar_1_desc: Provides a unified look and feel across all components, minimizing user learning costs.
guide_std_pillar_2_desc: Ensures equal access for all users through compliance with WAI-ARIA and full keyboard support.
guide_std_pillar_3_desc: Makes responses to operations clear, correctly conveying system and user states.
guide_visualization_desc: Guidelines for using charts and statistical data components.
internal_category_desc: These are foundational building-block components used internally across multiple other components. They are not intended for direct use in application code, but understanding them helps in contributing to or extending wimui.
internal_category_note: These components are internal implementation details. Prefer using the higher-level public components that wrap them.
internal_comp_base_list_role: Standardizes the layout and interaction states for list-like items used in Menus, Selects, and TreeViews.
internal_comp_field_template_role: Standardizes label placement, required markings, and error message display for all form-related components.
internal_comp_indicator_base_role: Standardizes the visual style and animation for status dots and badges.
internal_comp_input_base_role: Standardizes the visual container, icon slots (Prefix/Suffix), and clear button behavior for text-based inputs.
internal_comp_overlay_base_role: Standardizes the portal-based rendering, backdrop, and focus management for Dialogs, Drawers, and BottomSheets.
internal_comp_status_content_role: Standardizes the layout for results and empty states, including icon, title, and description.
loading_location_center: Center of screen or covering a container
loading_location_inline: Inside buttons, next to text, or small areas
loading_purpose_data_fetching: Waiting for data fetch or heavy processing
loading_spinner_vs_loader_desc: Both indicate a loading state, but their roles and display areas differ.
principle_animation_desc: Animations for component appearing/disappearing should be implemented via the Tier 1 Transition component rather than individual CSS definitions.
principle_dom_desc: When rendering elements like overlays or modals outside the DOM tree, use the Tier 1 Portal component instead of createPortal directly to unify portal logic.
principle_transition_desc: Animation behavior uses common transition classes (fade, scale, slide-*, etc.) defined in transition.scss to provide a consistent UX across the library.
qa_summary_desc: An overview of wimui's quality assurance, documentation, and test stability.
qa_summary_doc1_desc: The `Architecture/Standardization Overview` page has been created, enabling an overview of design philosophy and implementation rules in one place.
qa_summary_doc2_desc: Accessibility translation keys have been consolidated in `common.json`, enabling consistent aria-labels across the entire library.
qa_summary_fix1_desc: Standardized `react-i18next` mocks globally in `test-setup.ts`. Supports variable interpolation (`{{month}}`) and removed duplicate mock definitions from many test files.
qa_summary_fix2_desc: Updated queries like `getByLabelText` to use case-insensitive regular expressions (e.g., `/Close/i`) for resilience against minor translation label changes.
qa_summary_fix3_desc: Added `waitFor` handling in components with animations or state updates (`ToggleGroup`, `Transition`, etc.).
qa_summary_fix4_desc: Corrected property name and class name inconsistencies in `ColorPicker`, `Menu`, `Kbd`, and others.
qa_summary_section1_desc: All 23 previously failing unit tests have been resolved. All **152 test files** and **530 test cases** now pass.
qa_summary_section2_desc: The following tools have been introduced to maintain quality:
qa_summary_tool1_desc: `npm run test:coverage` visualizes code coverage (using v8).
qa_summary_tool2_desc: `npm run test:check` (`scripts/generate-missing-tests-report.cjs`) automatically detects components without test files.
qa_summary_tool3_desc: `npm run test:report` runs coverage measurement and automated checks together.
viz_area_data_desc: Changes in total values, emphasizing volume over time.
viz_axis_labeling_desc: Always clearly label units and axes.
viz_bar_data_desc: Categorical data, rankings, comparing differences between items.
viz_color_minimal_desc: Minimize colors; focus on differentiating items via PCCS palette.
viz_funnel_data_desc: Process reduction stages, conversion rate visualization.
viz_gauge_data_desc: Current position relative to a single target, achievement level.
viz_heatmap_data_desc: Multi-variable matrix density, behavioral pattern visualization.
viz_legend_pos_desc: Fine-tune legend placement so it doesn't crowd the chart.
viz_line_data_desc: Time series, continuous changes, trend analysis.
viz_multi_series_total_trend: Trends in totals for multiple series
viz_not_only_color_desc: Don't rely solely on color; use tooltips and labels for clarity.
viz_pie_data_desc: Composition ratios, market share, portion of total.
viz_scatter_data_desc: Correlation between 2 variables, outlier detection, distribution checks.
viz_selection_criteria_desc: Select the optimal chart for the story you want to tell and the nature of your data.
viz_treemap_data_desc: Hierarchical data ratios, contribution to the whole.
